title: dryopteris dilatata subsp. alexeenkoana
intro: Dryopteris dilatata subsp. alexeenkoana, also known as the broad buckler fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris dilatata species, a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of broad buckler fern include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water or using it in tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
